description: anvilml-server: EventBroadcaster
context: Create src/ws/broadcaster.rs: EventBroadcaster{sender: tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<Arc<WsEvent>>}. new(capacity:usize). fn send(&self, event:WsEvent) wrapping in Arc and ignoring SendError (no subscribers is fine). fn subscribe(&self)->broadcast::Receiver<Arc<WsEvent>>. cargo test -p anvilml-server -- broadcaster exits 0: subscribe, send, receive equal event; send with no subscribers does not error.

description: anvilml-server: WebSocket /v1/events handler
context: Add broadcaster: Arc<EventBroadcaster> to AppState (capacity from cfg.limits.ws_broadcast_capacity). Create src/ws/handler.rs: ws_events(WebSocketUpgrade, State)->on_upgrade. On connect subscribe; forward each Arc<WsEvent> as Message::Text(json). On RecvError::Lagged close with code 1008. No history replay. Wire GET /v1/events. Add tokio-tungstenite dev-dep. cargo test -p anvilml-server --features mock-hardware -- ws exits 0: connect, broadcast a test event, assert received as JSON text.

description: anvilml-server: WS keepalive ping every 30s
context: In ws/handler.rs add a ping task: tokio::time::interval(30s) sending Message::Ping(vec![]) to the socket; on send error end the connection. Run alongside the broadcast-forward task via tokio::select! so either ending closes the socket. cargo test -p anvilml-server --features mock-hardware -- ws still exits 0 (no regression).

description: anvilml-server: system.stats tick task (5s broadcast)
context: Create src/ws/stats_tick.rs: spawn_system_stats_tick(state)->JoinHandle. Every 5s build SystemStatsEvent: per-device vram from AppState.hardware (used 0 until worker reports exist), host ram via sysinfo; broadcaster.send(WsEvent::SystemStats). Call it from build_router setup or main startup. Verify via next task.

description: anvilml: start stats tick at startup; verify live WS stream
context: In main.rs after AppState built, call spawn_system_stats_tick(state.clone()). Ensure broadcaster + tick are live for the bound server. Verify: cargo run --features mock-hardware, then in another shell `websocat ws://127.0.0.1:8488/v1/events` (or a browser WS console) shows a system.stats JSON frame arriving every ~5 seconds with event='system.stats' and a timestamp.

description: anvilml: add real-hardware lint steps to rust-linux and rust-windows CI jobs
context: Edit .github/workflows/ci.yml only. In rust-linux job add step after Real-hardware compile check: name Real-hardware lint, run cargo clippy --bin anvilml -- -D warnings. In rust-windows job add step after Real-hardware compile check: name Real-hardware lint, run cargo clippy --bin anvilml -- -D warnings. No features flag on either. All existing jobs and steps preserved unchanged. Acceptance: grep -c "Real-hardware lint" .github/workflows/ci.yml returns 2.

description: anvilml: introduce [workspace.dependencies] and upgrade all external deps to current stable
context: Introduce [workspace.dependencies] in root Cargo.toml. Use mcp-rust-docs to resolve the current stable version of every external dependency currently declared across all per-crate Cargo.toml files. Populate [workspace.dependencies] with those current versions and their required features. Update every per-crate Cargo.toml to reference shared deps via { workspace = true } and remove the now-redundant inline version strings. Internal path deps (anvilml-core etc) are unchanged. cargo build --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-registry: fix db::open to create missing database file
context: P7-C1 upgraded sqlx; if still <0.9 after P7-C1, upgrade to current stable first. Fix db::open in crates/anvilml-registry/src/db.rs: replace SqlitePoolOptions::connect(path_str) with SqliteConnectOptions::new().filename(path).create_if_missing(true), then connect_with(opts). Add SqliteConnectOptions import from sqlx::sqlite. Remove fs::File::create pre-creation workaround from crates/anvilml-server/tests/api_models.rs setup_test_env. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0; delete anvilml.db then cargo run exits without DB panic.

description: anvilml-hardware: explicit detector warnings + Vulkan extension fix
context: Audit every silent Ok(vec![])/unwrap_or_default() discard in crates/anvilml-hardware/src/ (vulkan.rs, dxgi.rs, sysfs.rs, nvml.rs, lib.rs); replace each with tracing::warn!(detector, error). In vulkan.rs fix root cause: remove KHR_driver_properties + EXT_memory_budget from create_instance enabled_extension_names (device-level extensions; AMD ICD rejects them causing silent DXGI fallback); pass empty slice; guard properties2 calls with per-device enumerate_device_extension_properties check. cargo clippy --workspace exits 0. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-registry + anvilml-server: silent error discard fixes
context: Audit silent discards outside anvilml-hardware. In crates/anvilml-registry/src/scanner.rs scan_dirs: replace Err(_)=>continue on walkdir entry errors and metadata() errors with tracing::warn!(path, error); replace canonicalize().unwrap_or_else(|_|...) with a warn+fallback. In crates/anvilml-server/src/handlers/models.rs: in list_models Err(_e) arm add tracing::error!(error=%e) and return proper JSON error body {error,message} at 500; in get_model Err(_e) arm add tracing::error!(error=%e). cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings exits 0. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml: fix OS field blank and stray colon in --print-hardware output
context: Two bugs in backend/src/main.rs print_hardware_table. (1) OS line: format string passes " ".repeat(50-8) instead of hw.host.os — the field is never rendered; also has a literal trailing colon after the placeholder. Fix: println!("|| OS:          {}", hw.host.os). (2) populate_host_info in crates/anvilml-hardware/src/lib.rs: sysinfo 0.32 System::name() returns Some("") on Windows; replace with System::long_os_version().or_else(|| System::name()).unwrap_or_else(|| "Unknown".to_string()). cargo run -- --print-hardware shows a non-empty OS string without a stray colon.

description: anvilml: upgrade thiserror to 2.x and sha2 to 0.11.x
context: thiserror: bump [workspace.dependencies] from 1.x to 2.x. AnvilError in error.rs implements Display/Error/From manually and does not use the derive macro, so no code changes needed — the dep is inert. sha2: bump from 0.10.x to 0.11.x. Usage is entirely local (Sha256::new/.update/.finalize/hex::encode in scanner.rs); no public Digest trait bounds exposed. Verify hex 0.4.3 still compiles with sha2 0.11 output (GenericArray is AsRef<[u8]>). cargo build --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-core: migrate toml dependency from 0.8.x to 1.x
context: toml 1.0 broke three sites: (1) config_load.rs: ConfigError::Toml(toml::de::Error) — update error type path; update From<toml::de::Error> impl. (2) config.rs tests: toml::to_string_pretty moved to toml::ser::to_string_pretty; update call sites. (3) backend/tests/config_reference.rs: same to_string_pretty move plus toml::Value usage — verify Value API unchanged. Bump [workspace.dependencies] toml from 0.8.x to 1.x. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0. cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings exits 0.

description: anvilml-server: migrate axum from 0.7.x to 0.8.x (+ tower 0.4→0.5)
context: axum 0.8 breaks handler extractor ordering and Router API; tower must move to 0.5 simultaneously. Consult mcp-rust-docs for axum 0.8 and tower 0.5 migration guides before touching any file. Audit every handler in crates/anvilml-server/src/handlers/ and ws/: State<Arc<AppState>> must be first extractor. Update Router::with_state calls and any MethodRouter usage. Update [workspace.dependencies] axum to 0.8.x and tower to 0.5.x. cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings exits 0. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-core: extend InferenceCaps with fp32, fp8, fp4, nvfp4 fields
context: Extend InferenceCaps in crates/anvilml-core/src/types/hardware.rs: fields fp32:bool, fp16:bool, bf16:bool, fp8:bool, fp4:bool, nvfp4:bool, flash_attention:bool — all #[serde(default)]. Update or_all_caps in crates/anvilml-hardware/src/lib.rs to OR all 7 fields. Fix every InferenceCaps struct literal in the workspace (lib.rs, hardware.rs, cpu.rs, mock.rs, device_db.rs) — add new fields defaulting false. Update print_hardware_table in backend/src/main.rs to show all 7 flags in order. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0. cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings exits 0.

description: anvilml-registry: migration 004_device_capabilities.sql
context: Create backend/migrations/004_device_capabilities.sql per the DDL in docs/SUPPORTED_DEVICES_DB.md (Migration DDL reference section). Columns: vendor_id, device_id, model_name, arch, fp32, fp16, bf16, fp8, fp4, nvfp4, flash_attn — all INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 except model_name/arch TEXT NOT NULL; PRIMARY KEY(vendor_id,device_id); UNIQUE INDEX idx_device_capabilities_pci. Extend test_open_creates_tables to assert device_capabilities in sqlite_master. cargo test -p anvilml-registry exits 0.

description: anvilml-registry: DeviceCapabilityStore upsert + get + seed
context: Create crates/anvilml-registry/src/device_store.rs. pub struct DeviceCapabilityRow{vendor_id:u16,device_id:u16,model_name:String,arch:String,fp32:bool,fp16:bool,bf16:bool,fp8:bool,fp4:bool,nvfp4:bool,flash_attn:bool}. DeviceCapabilityStore{pool}: new(pool); async upsert(&DeviceCapabilityRow)->Result<()> INSERT OR REPLACE; async get(u16,u16)->Result<Option<DeviceCapabilityRow>>; async seed(&[DeviceCapabilityRow])->Result<u64> single tx, returns count. i64<->u16; bool as i64/!=0. Re-export. Tests: roundtrip, miss, seed count, bool. cargo test -p anvilml-registry -- device_store exits 0.

description: anvilml-hardware: device_db SEED_ENTRIES from SUPPORTED_DEVICES_DB.md + resolve_caps_from_row
context: Rewrite crates/anvilml-hardware/src/device_db.rs per docs/SUPPORTED_DEVICES_DB.md. Build pub const SEED_ENTRIES:&[DeviceCapabilityEntry] from both tables (Y=true N=false); fields: vendor_id,device_id,model_name,arch,fp32,fp16,bf16,fp8,fp4,nvfp4,flash_attn. Remove lookup() and resolve_caps(). Add resolve_caps_from_row(dev:&mut GpuDevice,row:Option<&DeviceCapabilityRow>): hit sets name/arch/caps/sources=DeviceTable; miss preserves dev.name, default/Fallback, warn!. anvilml-registry={workspace=true}. Rewrite tests via SEED_ENTRIES.iter().find(). cargo test -p anvilml-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-hardware: detect_all_devices seeds and queries device_capabilities
context: Make detect_all_devices in crates/anvilml-hardware/src/lib.rs async, add pool:&SqlitePool param. At entry: DeviceCapabilityStore::new(pool.clone()).seed(&SEED_ENTRIES).await?. Per device: store.get(pci_vendor_id,pci_device_id).await?, pass result to resolve_caps_from_row. Update backend/src/main.rs call site: pass &db, add .await. Update mod tests to use #[tokio::test] + pool from anvilml_registry::db::open_in_memory(). Add pub async fn open_in_memory()->Result<SqlitePool,AnvilError> to crates/anvilml-registry/src/db.rs. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: backend: create backend/seeds/devices.sql seed file
context: Create backend/seeds/devices.sql. Header: "-- anvil:seed_table devices\n-- anvil:seed_strategy replace_all". Body: one INSERT OR REPLACE INTO devices(...) VALUES(...) per row from both tables in docs/SUPPORTED_DEVICES_DB.md (read the file; copy verbatim; Y=1 N=0). Columns match 004_device_capabilities.sql order: vendor_id,device_id,model_name,arch,fp32,fp16,bf16,fp8,fp4,nvfp4,flash_attn. No Rust code. Verify SQL is syntactically valid by inspection. File must be present at backend/seeds/devices.sql.

description: anvilml-registry: seed_loader — tracking table bootstrap + SHA256 comparison
context: Create crates/anvilml-registry/src/seed_loader.rs. Bootstrap "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS seed_history(filename TEXT PRIMARY KEY,sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,applied_at INTEGER NOT NULL)" on every run. Parse header directives "-- anvil:seed_table <name>" and "-- anvil:seed_strategy <replace_all|merge>" from .sql file header; reject file missing seed_table directive. Compute SHA256 of file bytes; compare to seed_history; skip if unchanged. pub async fn run(pool:&SqlitePool,seeds_dir:&Path)->Result<(),AnvilError>. Re-export from lib.rs. cargo test -p anvilml-registry -- seed exits 0.

description: anvilml-registry: seed_loader — execution engine for replace_all and merge strategies
context: Implement seed execution in crates/anvilml-registry/src/seed_loader.rs. replace_all: single transaction — DELETE FROM <seed_table>, execute each INSERT from file body, UPDATE seed_history with new sha256+applied_at. merge: single transaction — INSERT OR REPLACE statements only, UPDATE seed_history. Both skip when sha256 unchanged. Tests (>=5): sha256_skip, replace_all_applies, merge_preserves_rows, changed_sha256_reruns, missing_directive_errors. cargo test -p anvilml-registry -- seed exits 0 >=5 tests.

description: anvilml-hardware: replace SEED_ENTRIES startup seed with SeedLoader; remove SEED_ENTRIES const
context: Replace store.seed(&SEED_ENTRIES) in detect_all_devices in crates/anvilml-hardware/src/lib.rs with SeedLoader::run(pool,seeds_dir).await?. seeds_dir resolved from ServerConfig (add seeds_path:PathBuf defaulting to executable_dir/seeds). Remove SEED_ENTRIES const and its import from device_db.rs. Gate DeviceCapabilityStore::seed() with #[cfg(any(test,feature="seed-util"))]. Update detect_all_devices mod tests to copy backend/seeds/devices.sql to a tempdir and pass it as seeds_dir. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-hardware + backend: fix name resolution priority and display
context: Fix name resolution in crates/anvilml-hardware/src/device_db.rs resolve_caps_from_row and backend/src/main.rs print_hardware_table. Hit path: if dev.name is empty or generic set dev.name=row.model_name; else preserve dev.name and set dev.db_group_name=Some(row.model_name). Miss path: if dev.name empty set dev.name=format!("Unknown GPU (0x{:04X}:0x{:04X})",vendor_id,device_id). Display: "{name} ({db_group_name})" when db_group_name differs from name, else "{name}". cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0.
